chunks.page_summary: This document outlines the steps for Modbus TCP/IP integration with Micro820 controllers, covering initial setup requirements and Rheonics Control Panel configuration.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Modbus TCP/IP Integration with Micro820
Modified on: Fri, 16 Jun, 2023 at 6:59 PM

1. GETTING STARTED

1.1. Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP or DVM w/ Firmware V03.20/0 or higher
1.2. Allen Bradley Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB
1.3. Rockwell Automation CCW Version 12.00.00 (includes RSLinx Classic Lite 4.11.00 CPR 9 SR 11.0)
1.4. Windows 10 64 bit
1.6. Ethernet Switch

2. Rheonics Control Panel Setup

2.1. Connect Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC (with RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL software installed) with an Ethernet Cable (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000300292-introducing-ethernet-connections-on-srnet
https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000300292-introducing-ethernet-%20connections-on-srnet ). We recommend using an Ethernet Switch.
2.2. For Static IP address go to the “Comm Configure tab” in the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL; in the Ethernet section click the dropdown menu in DHCP and select false (This will disable DHCP function)
key_facts: The document details Modbus TCP/IP integration with Micro820.
The document was last modified on June 16, 2023.

Required components for getting started include specific Rheonics sensors (SRV, SRD, DVP, DVM) with firmware V03.20/0 or higher, an Allen Bradley Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB, Rockwell Automation CCW Version 12.00.00 (including RSLinx Classic Lite 4.11.00 CPR 9 SR 11.0), Windows 10 64-bit, and an Ethernet Switch.
Connecting the Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC requires an Ethernet Cable.
It is recommended to use an Ethernet Switch for connections.

To configure a Static IP address, users must navigate to the 'Comm Configure tab' in the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL, access the Ethernet section, click the DHCP dropdown, and select 'false' to disable DHCP.
warnings_notes: Selecting 'false' for DHCP in the Rheonics Control Panel's Ethernet section will disable the DHCP function.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software version is 4.3.0.2.
Disabling DHCP allows for configuring a Static IP Address.
The Ethernet settings include MAC address, IP address, Subnet, Gateway IP, DNS, and Terminal Port.
The LCD display can be configured to show Viscosity, Density, and Temperature with specified resolutions.
Operational history includes RTC Time, Supply Voltage, Hardware Version, and various temperature exposures.
The configuration changes are applied by clicking the 'Update SMET' button.
The next setup step described is 'PLC Setup'.

warnings_notes: Remember to use valid network parameters when filling in Ethernet settings.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on configuring network settings for Modbus TCP/IP integration using Rheonics' CCWARC program and Rockwell Automation's RSLinx Classic software, including how to open the software and scan for network devices.
chunks.extracted_text:
This section explains how to navigate in the CCW software to configure the correct network settings. Rheonics provides a premade. CCWARC as a basic program for the Modbus TCP/IP integration. (If CCW software version 12.00.00 is not installed please follow this link https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-au/capabilities/industrial-automation-control/design-and-configuration-software.html for (https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-au/capabilities/industrial-automation-control/design-and-configuration-software.html for) download)

3.1. RS Linx Classic

1. Once the software has been installed open the RSLinx Classic software.
Figure 3. Opening Rslinx Classic

2. Scan all systems in the network. To do this, click on “Rswho” button.
Figure 4. Scan all network devices by pushing the” Rs Who” Button

3. Check for the following screen to be displayed:
key_facts: This section describes how to configure network settings within the CCW software for Modbus TCP/IP integration.
Rheonics provides a program called CCWARC for basic Modbus TCP/IP integration.
If CCW software version 12.00.00 is not installed, a download link to Rockwell Automation's website is provided.
The first step in configuration is to open the RSLinx Classic software after installation.
To scan all systems in the network, users must click the 'Rswho' button in RSLinx Classic.
The document expects a specific screen to be displayed after initiating the network scan, though the screen itself is not shown.
diagram_labels: Figure 4. Scan all network devices by pushing the” Rs Who” Button
warnings_notes: If CCW software version 12.00.00 is not installed, download it from https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-au/capabilities/industrial-automation-control/design-and-configuration-software.html

4. Connected Component Workbench
  1. Open the Connected Component Workbench (CCW) Application
key_facts: The RSWho function within RSLinx Classic Lite software is used to discover devices connected via Ethernet.
The discovery process reveals both a Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) and an unrecognized device.
The unrecognized device is identified as 'RH BJBW-B00-0000 SRV' with IP address 192.168.8.133, associated with a Rheonics SRV electronic sensor.
A Micro820 PLC (model 2080-LC20-20QBB) is found with IP address 192.168.8.134.
The next step in the procedure involves opening the Connected Component Workbench (CCW) Application.

key_facts: The page guides users to start 'Connected Components Workbench' software.
Users need to select 'Import project' from the 'file' tab within the CCW environment.
Rheonics offers an example project pre-configured for Modbus TCP/IP client communication.
The SMET is identified as operating in client mode.
The specific project file to import is named “RheonicsSRV_micro820_ModbusTCPIP.ccwarc”.
All project configurations become visible after importing the specified file.
Ethernet settings, including the IP address, are configurable and verifiable through the Ethernet option.

key_facts: The document illustrates the main configuration page for an Allen-Bradley Micro820 controller within a CCWARC project.
The Micro820 controller's general settings include its name, vendor, catalog ID, and project version.
Ethernet settings for the Micro820 controller allow for enabling/disabling the port, auto-negotiating speed and duplex mode, and configuring Internet Protocol (IP) settings.
IP settings can be obtained automatically via DHCP or manually configured with a static IP address, subnet mask, and gateway address.
The current manual IP configuration for the controller is 192.168.8.134 with a subnet mask of 255.255.255.0 and a gateway of 192.168.8.1.
An instruction is provided to configure the IP address of a Rheonics SRV sensor by navigating to the “Modbus_TCP” section in the Ladder program.
diagram_labels: Obtain IP address automatically using DHCP

key_facts: A ladder program is used for Modbus TCP/IP configuration.
Parameters in the Modbus TCP/IP configuration ladder program can be modified by right-clicking and selecting 'Variable Selector'.
The 'Variable Selector' context menu offers options like Cut, Copy, Paste, Delete, Cross Reference Browser, Properties, and Reset Element Visual Settings.
Global variable settings in Micro820 are used for Modbus TCP/IP configuration.

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the configuration of Modbus parameters using a 'Variable Selector' software interface, detailing specific settings for input registers and linking to an external support article for more information.
chunks.extracted_text:
Variable Selector
Name [empty] Type MODBUS2LOCPARA
Global Scope Micro820 Local Scope N/A
User Global Variables - Micro820 Local Variables - N/A System Variables - Micro820 I/O - Micro820 Defined Words
Name Alias Data Type Dimension Initial Value Comment Retained S
LOCAL_CFG MODBUS2L
TARGET_CFG MODBUS2T
LOCAL_ADDRES MODBUSLC
VISCOSITY_global REAL
DENSITY_GLOBAL REAL
TEMPERATURE_GLC REAL

Figure 13. Modbus parameters for edition

8. Open Local_CFG and set the parameters as need it. Below find the settings for the input registers but any of the Registers are available (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000397127-using-rheonics-file-for-settings-in-modbus (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000397127-using-rheonics-file-for-settings-in-modbus))

Variable Selector
Name LOCAL_CFG Type MODBUS2LOCPARA
Global Scope Micro820 Local Scope N/A
User Global Variables - Micro820 Local Variables - N/A System Variables - Micro820 I/O - Micro820 Defined Words
Name Alias Data Type Dimension Initial Value Comment Retained S
LOCAL_CFG MODBUS2L
  LOCAL_CFG.Ch UINT 4 Local Channel number
  LOCAL_CFG.Tr UDINT 1 0 = Trigger once, n = Cyclic Tric
  LOCAL_CFG.Cr USINT 4 Modbus command
  LOCAL_CFG.El UINT 20 No. of elements to Read/Write
TARGET_CFG MODBUS2T
LOCAL_ADDRES MODBUSLC
VISCOSITY_global REAL
DENSITY_GLOBAL REAL
TEMPERATURE_GLC REAL

4 is for Input Registers 20 the numbers of elements to cover the basic parameters.
key_facts: Modbus parameters are configured using a 'Variable Selector' interface within a software environment.
Users can open 'Local_CFG' to set parameters for Modbus communication.
The input register settings for 'Local_CFG' include 'Local Channel number', 'Trigger' (0 for once, n for cyclic), 'Modbus command', and 'No. of elements to Read/Write'.
A value of '4' for 'LOCAL_CFG.Cr' corresponds to the Modbus command for Input Registers.
A value of '20' for 'LOCAL_CFG.El' indicates the number of elements required to cover the basic parameters.
Further details on Modbus settings are available at the Rheonics support portal: https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000397127-using-rheonics-file-for-settings-in-modbus.

key_facts: The IP address of the Rheonics SMET device is configured using the “TARGET_Cfg” variable.

The Modbus TCP/IP configuration parameters include target Modbus data address, target node (IP) address (192.168.8.20), TCP port number, unit identifier, message timeout, connection timeout, and connection closing behavior.
Global variables for VISCOSITY, DENSITY, and TEMPERATURE are available and are of REAL data type.
The process involves building a program and downloading it into an Allen-Bradley Micro820 PLC.
The program being configured is named 'RheonicsSRV_modbustcp'.
The final step for program deployment is to select the 'Download with Values' option.
The Connected Components Workbench Standard Edition software is used for PLC programming.

key_facts: The page describes the process of downloading a configuration to an Allen-Bradley Micro820 PLC.
Downloading a project to the controller will overwrite any existing content.
The controller's mode will be switched to Program Mode during the download operation.
After download, the controller can be changed to Remote Run mode to execute the project.
IP address settings for the controller can be set to obtain an address automatically via DHCP or configured manually.
The Ethernet port supports auto-negotiation for speed and duplex mode.
diagram_labels: Obtain IP address automatically using DHCP
warnings_notes: The project in the controller will be overwritten with current project content.
Controller will be switched to Program Mode to perform the operation.
Download is complete. Change the controller to Remote Run to execute controller project?
